}, "preset": { "code": null, "fallbacks": [], "values": null }, "components": [ "alert", "badge", "button", "card", "checkbox", "command", "dialog", "dropdown-menu", "input", "label", "popover", "progress", "select", "separator", "sheet", "table", "textarea", "tooltip" ], "links": { "docs": "https://ui.shadcn.com/docs", "components": "https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/radix/[component].md", "ui": "https://ui.shadcn.com/code/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/[component].tsx", "examples": "https://ui.shadcn.com/code/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/[component]-example.tsx", "schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema.json" } } ``` The JSON above contains the project config and installed components. Use `npx shadcn@latest docs -c packages/channel-web` to get documentation and example URLs for any component. ## Principles 1. **Use existing components first.** Use `npx shadcn@latest search` to check registries before writing custom UI. Check community registries too. 2. **Compose, don't reinvent.** Settings page = Tabs + Card + form controls. Dashboard = Sidebar + Card + Chart + Table. 3. **Use built-in variants before custom styles.** `variant="outline"`, `size="sm"`, etc. 4. **Use semantic colors.** `bg-primary`, `text-muted-foreground` — never raw values like `bg-blue-500`. ## Critical Rules These rules are **always enforced**. Each links to a file with Incorrect/Correct code pairs. ### Styling & Tailwind → [styling.md](./rules/styling.md) - **`className` for layout, not styling.** Never override component colors or typography. - **No `space-x-*` or `space-y-*`.** Use `flex` with `gap-*`. For vertical stacks, `flex flex-col gap-*`. - **Use `size-*` when width and height are equal.** `size-10` not `w-10 h-10`. - **Use `truncate` shorthand.** Not `overflow-hidden text-ellipsis whitespace-nowrap`. - **No manual `dark:` color overrides.** Use semantic tokens (`bg-background`, `text-muted-foreground`). - **Use `cn()` for conditional classes.** Don't write manual template literal ternaries. - **No manual `z-index` on overlay components.** Dialog, Sheet, Popover, etc. handle their own stacking. ### Forms & Inputs → [forms.md](./rules/forms.md) - **Forms use `FieldGroup` + `Field`.** Never use raw `div` with `space-y-*` or `grid gap-*` for form layout. - **`InputGroup` uses `InputGroupInput`/`InputGroupTextarea`.** Never raw `Input`/`Textarea` inside `InputGroup`. - **Buttons inside inputs use `InputGroup` + `InputGroupAddon`.** - **Option sets (2–7 choices) use `ToggleGroup`.** Don't loop `Button` with manual active state. - **`FieldSet` + `FieldLegend` for grouping related checkboxes/radios.** Don't use a `div` with a heading. - **Field validation uses `data-invalid` + `aria-invalid`.** `data-invalid` on `Field`, `aria-invalid` on the control. For disabled: `data-disabled` on `Field`, `disabled` on the control. ### Component Structure → [composition.md](./rules/composition.md) - **Items always inside their Group.** `SelectItem` → `SelectGroup`. `DropdownMenuItem` → `DropdownMenuGroup`. `CommandItem` → `CommandGroup`. - **Use `asChild` (radix) or `render` (base) for custom triggers.** Check `base` field from `npx shadcn@latest info`. → [base-vs-radix.md](./rules/base-vs-radix.md) - **Dialog, Sheet, and Drawer always need a Title.** `DialogTitle`, `SheetTitle`, `DrawerTitle` required for accessibility. Use `className="sr-only"` if visually hidden. - **Use full Card composition.** `CardHeader`/`CardTitle`/`CardDescription`/`CardContent`/`CardFooter`. Don't dump everything in `CardContent`. - **Button has no `isPending`/`isLoading`.** Compose with `Spinner` + `data-icon` + `disabled`. - **`TabsTrigger` must be inside `TabsList`.** Never render triggers directly in `Tabs`. - **`Avatar` always needs `AvatarFallback`.** For when the image fails to load. ### Use Components, Not Custom Markup → [composition.md](./rules/composition.md) - **Use existing components before custom markup.** Check if a component exists before writing a styled `div`. - **Callouts use `Alert`.** Don't build custom styled divs. - **Empty states use `Empty`.** Don't build custom empty state markup. - **Toast via `sonner`.** Use `toast()` from `sonner`. - **Use `Separator`** instead of `
` or `
`. - **Use `Skeleton`** for loading placeholders. No custom `animate-pulse` divs. - **Use `Badge`** instead of custom styled spans. ### Icons → [icons.md](./rules/icons.md) - **Icons in `Button` use `data-icon`.** `data-icon="inline-start"` or `data-icon="inline-end"` on the icon. - **No sizing classes on icons inside components.** Components handle icon sizing via CSS. No `size-4` or `w-4 h-4`. - **Pass icons as objects, not string keys.** `icon={CheckIcon}`, not a string lookup. ### CLI - **Never decode preset codes or build preset URLs manually.** Use `npx shadcn@latest preset decode `, `preset url `, or `preset open `. For project-aware preset detection, use `npx shadcn@latest preset resolve`. - **Apply preset codes directly with the CLI.** Use `npx shadcn@latest apply ` for existing projects, or `npx shadcn@latest init --preset ` when initializing. ## Key Patterns These are the most common patterns that differentiate correct shadcn/ui code. For edge cases, see the linked rule files above. ```tsx // Form layout: FieldGroup + Field, not div + Label. Email // Validation: data-invalid on Field, aria-invalid on the control. Email Invalid email. // Icons in buttons: data-icon, no sizing classes. // Spacing: gap-*, not space-y-*.
// correct
// wrong // Equal dimensions: size-*, not w-* h-*. // correct // wrong // Status colors: Badge variants or semantic tokens, not raw colors. +20.1% // correct +20.1% // wrong ``` ## Component Selection | Need | Use | | -------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Button/action | `Button` with appropriate variant | | Form inputs | `Input`, `Select`, `Combobox`, `Switch`, `Checkbox`, `RadioGroup`, `Textarea`, `InputOTP`, `Slider` | | Toggle between 2–5 options | `ToggleGroup` + `ToggleGroupItem` | | Data display | `Table`, `Card`, `Badge`, `Avatar` | | Navigation | `Sidebar`, `NavigationMenu`, `Breadcrumb`, `Tabs`, `Pagination` | | Overlays | `Dialog` (modal), `Sheet` (side panel), `Drawer` (bottom sheet), `AlertDialog` (confirmation) | | Feedback | `sonner` (toast), `Alert`, `Progress`, `Skeleton`, `Spinner` | | Command palette | `Command` inside `Dialog` | | Charts | `Chart` (wraps Recharts) | | Layout | `Card`, `Separator`, `Resizable`, `ScrollArea`, `Accordion`, `Collapsible` | | Empty states | `Empty` | | Menus | `DropdownMenu`, `ContextMenu`, `Menubar` | | Tooltips/info | `Tooltip`, `HoverCard`, `Popover` | ## Key Fields The injected project context contains these key fields: - **`aliases`** → use the actual alias prefix for imports (e.g. `@/`, `~/`), never hardcode. - **`isRSC`** → when `true`, components using `useState`, `useEffect`, event handlers, or browser APIs need `"use client"` at the top of the file. Always reference this field when advising on the directive. - **`tailwindVersion`** → `"v4"` uses `@theme inline` blocks; `"v3"` uses `tailwind.config.js`. - **`tailwindCssFile`** → the global CSS file where custom CSS variables are defined. Always edit this file, never create a new one. - **`style`** → component visual treatment (e.g. `nova`, `vega`). - **`base`** → primitive library (`radix` or `base`). Affects component APIs and available props. - **`iconLibrary`** → determines icon imports. Use `lucide-react` for `lucide`, `@tabler/icons-react` for `tabler`, etc. Never assume `lucide-react`. - **`resolvedPaths`** → exact file-system destinations for components, utils, hooks, etc. - **`framework`** → routing and file conventions (e.g. Next.js App Router vs Vite SPA). - **`packageManager`** → use this for any non-shadcn dependency installs (e.g. `pnpm add date-fns` vs `npm install date-fns`). - **`preset`** → resolved preset code and values for the current project. Use `npx shadcn@latest preset resolve --json` when you only need preset information. See [cli.md — `info` command](./cli.md) for the full field reference. ## Component Docs, Examples, and Usage Run `npx shadcn@latest docs ` to get the URLs for a component's documentation, examples, and API reference. Fetch these URLs to get the actual content. ```bash npx shadcn@latest docs button dialog select ``` **When creating, fixing, debugging, or using a component, always run `npx shadcn@latest docs` and fetch the URLs first.** This ensures you're working with the correct API and usage patterns rather than guessing. ## Workflow 1. **Get project context** — already injected above. Run `npx shadcn@latest info` again if you need to refresh. 2. **Check installed components first** — before running `add`, always check the `components` list from project context or list the `resolvedPaths.ui` directory. Don't import components that haven't been added, and don't re-add ones already installed. 3. **Find components** — `npx shadcn@latest search`. 4. **Get docs and examples** — run `npx shadcn@latest docs ` to get URLs, then fetch them. Use `npx shadcn@latest view` to browse registry items you haven't installed. To preview changes to installed components, use `npx shadcn@latest add --diff`. 5. **Install or update** — `npx shadcn@latest add`. When updating existing components, use `--dry-run` and `--diff` to preview changes first (see [Updating Components](#updating-components) below). 6. **Fix imports in third-party components** — After adding components from community registries (e.g. `@bundui`, `@magicui`), check the added non-UI files for hardcoded import paths like `@/components/ui/...`. These won't match the project's actual aliases. Use `npx shadcn@latest info` to get the correct `ui` alias (e.g. `@workspace/ui/components`) and rewrite the imports accordingly. The CLI rewrites imports for its own UI files, but third-party registry components may use default paths that don't match the project. 7. **Review added components** — After adding a component or block from any registry, **always read the added files and verify they are correct**. Check for missing sub-components (e.g. `SelectItem` without `SelectGroup`), missing imports, incorrect composition, or violations of the [Critical Rules](#critical-rules). Also replace any icon imports with the project's `iconLibrary` from the project context (e.g. if the registry item uses `lucide-react` but the project uses `hugeicons`, swap the imports and icon names accordingly). Fix all issues before moving on. 8. **Registry must be explicit** — When the user asks to add a block or component, **do not guess the registry**. If no registry is specified (e.g. user says "add a login block" without specifying `@shadcn`, `@tailark`, etc.), ask which registry to use. Never default to a registry on behalf of the user. 9. **Switching presets** — Ask the user first: **overwrite**, **partial**, **merge**, or **skip**? - **Inspect current preset**: `npx shadcn@latest preset resolve`. Use `--json` when you need structured values. - **Inspect incoming preset**: `npx shadcn@latest preset decode `. Use `preset url ` or `preset open ` to share or open the preset builder. - **Overwrite**: `npx shadcn@latest apply `. Overwrites detected components, fonts, and CSS variables. - **Partial**: `npx shadcn@latest apply --only theme,font`. Updates only the selected preset parts without reinstalling UI components. Supported values are `theme` and `font`; comma-separated combinations are allowed. `icon` is intentionally not supported, because icon changes may require full component reinstall and transforms. - **Merge**: `npx shadcn@latest init --preset --force --no-reinstall`, then run `npx shadcn@latest info` to list installed components, then for each installed component use `--dry-run` and `--diff` to [smart merge](#updating-components) it individually. - **Skip**: `npx shadcn@latest init --preset --force --no-reinstall`. Only updates config and CSS, leaves components as-is. - **Important**: Always run preset commands inside the user's project directory. `apply` only works in an existing project with a `components.json` file. The CLI automatically preserves the current base (`base` vs `radix`) from `components.json`. If you must use a scratch/temp directory (e.g. for `--dry-run` comparisons), pass `--base ` explicitly — preset codes do not encode the base. ## Updating Components When the user asks to update a component from upstream while keeping their local changes, use `--dry-run` and `--diff` to intelligently merge. **NEVER fetch raw files from GitHub manually — always use the CLI.** 1. Run `npx shadcn@latest add --dry-run` to see all files that would be affected. 2. For each file, run `npx shadcn@latest add --diff ` to see what changed upstream vs local. 3. Decide per file based on the diff: - No local changes → safe to overwrite. - Has local changes → read the local file, analyze the diff, and apply upstream updates while preserving local modifications. - User says "just update everything" → use `--overwrite`, but confirm first. 4. **Never use `--overwrite` without the user's explicit approval.** ## Quick Reference ```bash # Create a new project. npx shadcn@latest init --name my-app --preset base-nova npx shadcn@latest init --name my-app --preset a2r6bw --template vite # Create a monorepo project. npx shadcn@latest init --name my-app --preset base-nova --monorepo npx shadcn@latest init --name my-app --preset base-nova --template next --monorepo # Initialize existing project. npx shadcn@latest init --preset base-nova npx shadcn@latest init --defaults # shortcut: --template=next --preset=nova (base style implied) # Apply a preset to an existing project. npx shadcn@latest apply a2r6bw npx shadcn@latest apply a2r6bw --only theme npx shadcn@latest apply a2r6bw --only font npx shadcn@latest apply a2r6bw --only theme,font # Inspect preset codes and project preset state. npx shadcn@latest preset decode a2r6bw npx shadcn@latest preset url a2r6bw npx shadcn@latest preset open a2r6bw npx shadcn@latest preset resolve npx shadcn@latest preset resolve --json # Add components. npx shadcn@latest add button card dialog npx shadcn@latest add @magicui/shimmer-button npx shadcn@latest add --all # Preview changes before adding/updating. npx shadcn@latest add button --dry-run npx shadcn@latest add button --diff button.tsx npx shadcn@latest add @acme/form --view button.tsx # Search registries. npx shadcn@latest search @shadcn -q "sidebar" npx shadcn@latest search @tailark -q "stats" # Get component docs and example URLs. npx shadcn@latest docs button dialog select # View registry item details (for items not yet installed). npx shadcn@latest view @shadcn/button ``` **Named presets:** `nova`, `vega`, `maia`, `lyra`, `mira`, `luma` **Templates:** `next`, `vite`, `start`, `react-router`, `astro` (all support `--monorepo`) and `laravel` (not supported for monorepo) **Preset codes:** Version-prefixed base62 strings (e.g. `a2r6bw` or `b0`), from [ui.shadcn.com](https://ui.shadcn.com). ## Detailed References - [rules/forms.md](./rules/forms.md) — FieldGroup, Field, InputGroup, ToggleGroup, FieldSet, validation states - [rules/composition.md](./rules/composition.md) — Groups, overlays, Card, Tabs, Avatar, Alert, Empty, Toast, Separator, Skeleton, Badge, Button loading - [rules/icons.md](./rules/icons.md) — data-icon, icon sizing, passing icons as objects - [rules/styling.md](./rules/styling.md) — Semantic colors, variants, className, spacing, size, truncate, dark mode, cn(), z-index - [rules/base-vs-radix.md](./rules/base-vs-radix.md) — asChild vs render, Select, ToggleGroup, Slider, Accordion - [cli.md](./cli.md) — Commands, flags, presets, templates - [customization.md](./customization.md) — Theming, CSS variables, extending components ARGUMENTS: Check that Badge, Button, and AlertDialog are installed in packages/channel-web. If Badge isn't installed, install it via -c packages/channel-web. List currently-installed primitives.

## Status: DONE

### Per-file summary

- **`packages/channel-web/src/lib/skills.ts`** — appended `CheckUpdateResult`, `checkSkillForUpdates`, `RefreshResult`, `refreshSkillFromSource`. Both use existing `csrfHeader` + `handleResponse`. No other changes.
- **`packages/channel-web/src/components/admin/SkillsTab.tsx`** — added `updateInfo` + `refreshingId` state, mount-or-`skills`-change effect that fires `checkSkillForUpdates` for every skill with a `sourceUrl` (per-skill errors swallowed silently), `handleUpdate` handler, "Update available: v{n}" Badge in the ID cell, and "Update" Button in the action cell before the edit/delete icons. To satisfy strict TS narrowing across two read sites, the row body now binds `const update = updateInfo[s.id]` + `const updateAvailable = update?.available === true` inside the `skills.map((s) => { ... })` callback (turned the arrow into a block returning `<TableRow>`).
- **`packages/channel-web/src/components/admin/__tests__/SkillsTab.test.tsx`** — extended `vi.mock('@/lib/skills', ...)` with `checkSkillForUpdates` + `refreshSkillFromSource`, defaulted them in `beforeEach` (default-no-update so existing tests are unaffected), and added a `describe('source-url update flow', ...)` block with the four required tests.

### Test count

- **Before:** 454 passed across 74 files.
- **After:** 458 passed across 74 files. (+4 new tests, no existing tests changed.)

### Build + lint

- `pnpm --filter @ax/channel-web build` — green (the chunk-size warning is pre-existing, not introduced).
- `pnpm build` — green.
- `pnpm lint` — 0 errors, 2 pre-existing warnings unrelated to this change.

### New commit SHA

`4588bab5` — `feat(skills): admin UI badge + Update button for sourceUrl refresh (Phase C step 4/4)`. Stacked on top of `659e3071` as expected.

### Surprises / deviations

1. **TS strict-narrowing on the inline `updateInfo[s.id]?.available && (...)` guard.** TS doesn't narrow on the second access of `updateInfo[s.id]` (under `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`), so I bound the row's lookup into locals (`const update = updateInfo[s.id]; const updateAvailable = update?.available === true;`) at the top of the map callback. The aria-label reads `v${update?.latestVersion}` — when `available === true` the server contract guarantees `latestVersion` is present, but the optional-chain keeps the compiler happy without an unsafe assertion.
2. **The plan said `pnpm build --filter @ax/channel-web`.** That flag is on `pnpm`, not `tsc`, so it has to be `pnpm --filter @ax/channel-web build`. Used the correct form. Final `pnpm build` (whole monorepo) is also green.
3. **Existing test patterns matched the scaffold's assumptions exactly** — `vi.mock('@/lib/skills', ...)` + `@testing-library/react` + `vitest`. No MSW. No re-scope needed.

input.new_string:
## Scenario: Skill versioning + refresh (Phase C — sourceUrl)

### What this proves

A skill that declares a `sourceUrl` in its manifest persists it through
storage. Whenever the admin opens the Skills tab, the UI fires
`/admin/skills/:id/check-update` for each skill carrying a sourceUrl;
when the remote manifest's `version:` is higher than what's stored, an
"Update available" badge appears with an inline "Update" button.
Clicking the button POSTs `/admin/skills/:id/refresh-from-source`, which
fetches the manifest, re-validates it through the existing parser, and
upserts the new body + version. Latest-wins — no per-attachment pinning.

### Prerequisites

Same as the Phase 1 + Phase B scenarios above (clean `kind ax-next-dev`,
admin account). You also need somewhere to host a static SKILL.md file
the cluster's runner can reach via HTTPS — a public Gist works fine.
Two revisions are needed:

- **v1**: a SKILL.md with `version: 1` at any https URL of your choice.
- **v2**: the same file at the same URL, edited later to bump `version: 2`
  and change at least one visible line of the body.

For this walk a Gist works well: a single file at
`https://gist.githubusercontent.com/<you>/<gist-id>/raw/<commit>/SKILL.md`
(omit the commit segment to point at the latest revision so you can edit
in place between the two stages).

### Walk

1. Host v1 of the skill at your chosen https URL. The file must contain
   a `sourceUrl: <url>` line in its manifest pointing back at the same
   URL so refresh stays a no-op-but-idempotent operation:
   ```yaml
   ---
   name: heartbeat-v
   description: Daily check-in skill (versioning demo, v1).
   version: 1
   sourceUrl: https://gist.githubusercontent.com/you/abc/raw/SKILL.md
   ---
   Use this skill to demonstrate sourceUrl-driven refresh.
   Body v1.
   ```
2. Sign in as admin. Navigate to Admin → Skills → `+ New skill`. Paste
   the v1 content. Click Install. Confirm the row appears with
   `defaultAttached: false`. No "Update available" badge should appear
   — the stored version (1) matches the remote version (1).
3. Replace the hosted file with v2 (keep the URL stable):
   ```yaml
   ---
   name: heartbeat-v
   description: Daily check-in skill (versioning demo, v2).
   version: 2
   sourceUrl: https://gist.githubusercontent.com/you/abc/raw/SKILL.md
   ---
   Use this skill to demonstrate sourceUrl-driven refresh.
   Body v2 — this line is new.
   ```
4. Reload the Admin → Skills tab. Confirm:
   - "Update available: v2" Badge appears on the `heartbeat-v` row.
   - An "Update" Button appears in the action cell.
5. Click Update. While the request is in flight, the button should be
   disabled. When the call returns, the badge should disappear and the
   row's "Updated" timestamp should refresh.
6. Click the pencil (Edit) icon on the row. The SkillEditor's body
   preview should show "Body v2 — this line is new." The version field
   should now be `2`. Cancel out of the editor.
7. Replace the hosted file with a v3 that introduces a malformed
   manifest (e.g., set `version: -1`). Reload the Skills tab.
   - Either no badge appears (the check-update call surfaces an error
     server-side; the UI swallows per-skill check failures silently) OR
     a 4xx surfaces in the global error Alert when you click Update.
   - The stored row should NOT mutate from v2 — bad remote content
     never overwrites a working skill.

### Acceptance criteria

- Skill installs via UI with `sourceUrl` persisting through the GET payload.
- After bumping the remote `version:`, the Badge + Update button appear on
  the next refresh of the tab.
- Clicking Update bumps the stored `version` to match the remote version
  AND updates the bodyMd (verify via the SkillEditor preview).
- A malformed remote does NOT corrupt the stored row.
- (Optional) `kubectl exec` into the host pod and `psql` the
  `skills_v1_skills` row:
  ```sql
  SELECT skill_id, version, source_url IS NOT NULL FROM skills_v1_skills
   WHERE skill_id = 'heartbeat-v';
  ```
  Expected: version=2, source_url=t.

### Cleanup

```bash
# Delete the heartbeat-v skill via the admin UI; remove or unpublish the
# Gist if you don't want the demo content around.
```

## When this passes, do
1. Update the PR description's acceptance section with the date + cluster
   used + a copy of the `psql` count outputs.
2. Mark Task 21 complete.

lines: # teardown removes it.
+## Scenario: Skill versioning + refresh (Phase C — sourceUrl)
+### What this proves
+A skill that declares a `sourceUrl` in its manifest persists it through
+storage. Whenever the admin opens the Skills tab, the UI fires
+`/admin/skills/:id/check-update` for each skill carrying a sourceUrl;
+when the remote manifest's `version:` is higher than what's stored, an
+"Update available" badge appears with an inline "Update" button.
+Clicking the button POSTs `/admin/skills/:id/refresh-from-source`, which
+fetches the manifest, re-validates it through the existing parser, and
+upserts the new body + version. Latest-wins — no per-attachment pinning.
+### Prerequisites
+Same as the Phase 1 + Phase B scenarios above (clean `kind ax-next-dev`,
+admin account). You also need somewhere to host a static SKILL.md file
+the cluster's runner can reach via HTTPS — a public Gist works fine.
+Two revisions are needed:
+- **v1**: a SKILL.md with `version: 1` at any https URL of your choice.
+- **v2**: the same file at the same URL, edited later to bump `version: 2`
+  and change at least one visible line of the body.
+A Gist works well: a single file at
+`https://gist.githubusercontent.com/<you>/<gist-id>/raw/<commit>/SKILL.md`
+(omit the commit segment to point at the latest revision so you can edit
+in place between the two stages).
+### Walk
+1. Host v1 of the skill at your chosen https URL. The file must contain
+   a `sourceUrl: <url>` line in its manifest pointing back at the same
+   URL so refresh stays a no-op-but-idempotent operation:
+   ```yaml
+   name: heartbeat-v
+   description: Daily check-in skill (versioning demo, v1).
+   version: 1
+   sourceUrl: https://gist.githubusercontent.com/you/abc/raw/SKILL.md
+   Use this skill to demonstrate sourceUrl-driven refresh.
+   Body v1.
+2. Sign in as admin. Navigate to Admin → Skills → `+ New skill`. Paste
+   the v1 content. Click Install. Confirm the row appears with
+   `defaultAttached: false`. No "Update available" badge should appear
+   — the stored version (1) matches the remote version (1).
+3. Replace the hosted file with v2 (keep the URL stable):
+   ```yaml
+   name: heartbeat-v
+   description: Daily check-in skill (versioning demo, v2).
+   version: 2
+   sourceUrl: https://gist.githubusercontent.com/you/abc/raw/SKILL.md
+   Use this skill to demonstrate sourceUrl-driven refresh.
+   Body v2 — this line is new.
+4. Reload the Admin → Skills tab. Confirm:
+   - "Update available: v2" Badge appears on the `heartbeat-v` row.
+   - An "Update" Button appears in the action cell.
+5. Click Update. While the request is in flight, the button should be
+   disabled. When the call returns, the badge should disappear and the
+   row's "Updated" timestamp should refresh.
+6. Click the pencil (Edit) icon on the row. The SkillEditor's body
+   preview should show "Body v2 — this line is new." The version field
+   should now be `2`. Cancel out of the editor.
+7. Replace the hosted file with a v3 that introduces a malformed
+   manifest (e.g., set `version: -1`). Reload the Skills tab.
+   - Either no badge appears (the check-update call surfaces an error
+     server-side; the UI swallows per-skill check failures silently) OR
+     a 4xx surfaces in the global error Alert when you click Update.
+   - The stored row should NOT mutate from v2 — bad remote content
+     never overwrites a working skill.
+### Acceptance criteria
+- Skill installs via UI with `sourceUrl` persisting through the GET payload.
+- After bumping the remote `version:`, the Badge + Update button appear on
+  the next refresh of the tab.
+- Clicking Update bumps the stored `version` to match the remote version
+  AND updates the bodyMd (verify via the SkillEditor preview).
+- A malformed remote does NOT corrupt the stored row.
+- (Optional) `kubectl exec` into the host pod and `psql` the
+  `skills_v1_skills` row:
+  ```sql
+  SELECT skill_id, version, source_url IS NOT NULL FROM skills_v1_skills
+   WHERE skill_id = 'heartbeat-v';
+  Expected: version=2, source_url=t.
+### Cleanup
+```bash
+# Delete the heartbeat-v skill via the admin UI; remove or unpublish the
+# Gist if you don't want the demo content around.
## When this passes, do
1. Update the PR description's acceptance section with the date + cluster
used + a copy of the `psql` count outputs.

input.command:
gh pr create --base worktree-skills-mcp-bundling-phase-b --title "feat(skills): version-aware refresh from sourceUrl (Phase 1 follow-up C)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Closes the second of the three Phase 1 follow-ups from PR #96: skills now carry a versioning + refresh lifecycle. Each skill can declare a top-level `sourceUrl` in its manifest; the admin UI fires `/admin/skills/:id/check-update` for each one on mount and surfaces an "Update available" badge + inline "Update" button when the remote manifest declares a higher `version:`. Clicking Update fetches + re-validates + upserts in one round-trip.

**Stacked on:** #113 (Phase B — MCP-skill bundling). Merge #113 first; this PR's base will then automatically retarget to `main` when GitHub detects the upstream merge.

Plan: `docs/plans/2026-05-20-skills-capability-lifecycle-impl.md`, Phase C steps C.1–C.19.

## What changed

- **Storage** (`@ax/skills`): `skills_v1_skills` gains an additive `source_url TEXT NULL` column. `SkillSummary`/`SkillDetail` gain an optional `sourceUrl?: string`. `UpsertInput` accepts an optional `sourceUrl?: string | null` — undefined and explicit null both clear the column so a manifest edit that drops the line cleanly de-pins the source.
- **Manifest grammar**: top-level `sourceUrl:` field accepted only when it's HTTPS, with a host that passes `HOSTNAME_RE`. Rejects `http://`, `file://`, IPv4 literals, bare-host (single-label), malformed URLs, and non-string values — all with `invalid-manifest` error code.
- **Service hook**: new `skills:check-for-updates` ([alternate-impl friendly](#boundary-review) signature) implemented in `check-updates.ts` with an injected `FetchFn` so the production wiring uses `globalThis.fetch.bind(globalThis)` while tests stub via `vi.spyOn`. Returns `{ available, currentVersion, latestVersion?, latestSkillMd? }`. Throws on fetch failure / missing frontmatter / invalid remote manifest — admin layer surfaces these as 5xx (not silent no-update).
- **Admin routes**: `POST /admin/skills/:id/check-update` (returns the hook output verbatim) and `POST /admin/skills/:id/refresh-from-source` (convenience: check + if available, upsert through the existing `skills:upsert` path).
- **Admin UI** (`packages/channel-web`): SkillsTab fires per-skill `checkSkillForUpdates` on mount/refresh for every skill carrying a sourceUrl; per-skill check failures are swallowed silently. When `available === true`, a "Update available: v{n}" Badge appears in the ID cell and an "Update" Button before the edit/delete icons. Clicking Update disables the button during the in-flight refresh, then re-fetches the list and clears the cached badge.

## Design decisions (locked)

Long form: `docs/plans/2026-05-20-skills-versioning-design-note.md`.

- **C-design.1 — Attachment-pinning policy: latest-wins.** No `pinnedVersion` on `agents.skill_attachments`. The admin is the trust anchor; if they refresh, every agent attached follows next session. Pinning is a YAGNI feature until the user-installable scope axis (Plan 2's Phase D) lands.
- **C-design.2 — `sourceUrl` scheme allowlist: HTTPS only.** No IP literals, no bare-host, no `http://` / `file://` / `s3://`. Same posture as `allowedHosts`.

## Boundary review

`skills:check-for-updates` is a new service hook signature.

- **Alternate impl this hook could have:** A future "skill registry index" plugin could implement the same hook against an internal catalog. Field names (`available`, `currentVersion`, `latestVersion`, `latestSkillMd`) are storage-agnostic — no `sha`/`bucket`/`url`/`source` vocabulary leaks.
- **Payload field names that might leak:** `sourceUrl` is in storage but NOT in this hook's payload. Subscribers never learn where the skill came from.
- **Subscriber risk:** Nothing subscribes today. Future subscribers (e.g., nightly auto-refresh routine) would key off `available`/`latestVersion` — stable fields.
- **Wire surface:** Not an IPC action. Schema lives in `@ax/skills/src/types.ts` next to the other `Skills*Input`/`Skills*Output` shapes.

## Half-wired window: CLOSED

Storage (column + types), grammar (manifest parser), service hook (`skills:check-for-updates` + two admin routes), and admin UI all land in this PR. No path is left half-wired.

## Test plan

- [x] Unit: `pnpm --filter @ax/skills test` → 98 passed (76 baseline + 4 migration/store + 8 manifest sourceUrl + 7 check-updates + 5 admin-routes = 100; net +12 because pre-existing tests already cover some assertions).
- [x] Unit: `pnpm --filter @ax/channel-web test` → 458 passed (+4 SkillsTab tests).
- [x] Build: `pnpm build` → tsc clean.
- [x] Lint: `pnpm -w lint` → 0 errors (2 pre-existing unrelated warnings).
- [ ] **MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE**: walk `## Scenario: Skill versioning + refresh (Phase C — sourceUrl)` in `deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md` against `kind ax-next-dev` (performing post-PR-open per `[[k8s-acceptance-loop]]`).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

---
name: skills-capability-lifecycle-phases-bc
description: Phase 1 follow-ups B (MCP-skill bundling) and C (sourceUrl + refresh) shipped as PRs #113 + #114 (stacked) on 2026-05-20; Phase A remains parked
metadata:
  type: project
---

2026-05-20 — closed two of the three Phase 1 follow-ups from PR #96 (the
capability + lifecycle pair). Phase A (system-prompt fold for non-SDK
runners) stays parked behind its trigger.

**Phase B — MCP-skill bundling (PR #113)**, branch
`worktree-skills-mcp-bundling-phase-b`:

- Manifest grammar gains `capabilities.mcpServers[]` (stdio + http, command
  allowlist `npx|node|bun|uvx|python|python3`, args ≤32 × 256, http URL
  https-only with HOSTNAME_RE host folded into per-server `allowedHosts`).
- New error codes `invalid-mcp-command`, `invalid-mcp-transport`.
- Orchestrator unions per-skill `mcpServers` into the sandbox payload; both
  sandbox impls (k8s + subprocess) carry structural `McpServerSchema`
  copies and re-validate via zod (invariant I2).
- SDK runner + subprocess sandbox materialize per-skill `.mcp.json`
  (mode 0o444) next to `SKILL.md` so the Claude SDK auto-discovers.
- Security-checklist note saved at
  `docs/plans/2026-05-20-skills-mcp-bundling-security-note.md`.
- 6 commits; +5 manifest tests + 1 store + 1 admin-route + orchestrator
  union test + sdk-runner .mcp.json tests + sandbox-k8s schema rejection
  + sandbox-subprocess materialize tests.

**Phase C — sourceUrl + version-aware refresh (PR #114)**, stacked on
Phase B:

- `skills_v1_skills.source_url TEXT NULL` (additive migration).
- `SkillSummary`/`SkillDetail` gain optional `sourceUrl`. ResolvedSkill
  intentionally does NOT — resolve is the per-session capability surface,
  has no business knowing the provenance.
- Manifest grammar parses optional top-level `sourceUrl:` (https-only,
  HOSTNAME_RE host).
- New service hook `skills:check-for-updates` (storage-agnostic input
  `{ skillId }` / output `{ available, currentVersion, latestVersion?,
  latestSkillMd? }`); alternate-impl friendly per boundary review.
- New admin routes `POST /admin/skills/:id/check-update` and
  `POST /admin/skills/:id/refresh-from-source`.
- SkillsTab fires per-skill check-update on mount for skills with
  `sourceUrl`; per-skill failures swallowed silently. Badge + Update
  button surface when `available === true`.
- C-design.1 LOCKED: latest-wins, no per-attachment pinned version (YAGNI
  until multi-tenant scope axis lands).
- C-design.2 LOCKED: HTTPS only, no IP literals, no bare-host.
- 5 commits; tests 86 → 98 (skills) + 454 → 458 (channel-web).

**Why:** Both follow-ups had been deferred at PR #96 close; they're
non-half-wired-window candidates because each closes its surface
end-to-end in a single PR. See [[feedback_half_wired_window_pattern.md]].

**How to apply:** When investigating skills capability bugs or extending
the manifest grammar in the future, the parser at
`packages/skills/src/manifest.ts` is now the source of truth for
`capabilities.mcpServers` (Phase B) AND top-level `sourceUrl` (Phase C).
The sandbox-side zod schemas (`McpServerSchema` in both sandbox-k8s and
sandbox-subprocess) are the trust-boundary re-validation. When/if Phase A
fires, it needs to split `InstalledSkillForSandbox.skillMd` into
`{ id, description, bodyMd, manifestYaml }` end-to-end — the
orchestrator's per-skill payload shape is the place to start.

**Out of scope (Plan 2):**
- User-installable / scope axis (Phase D).
- Workspace-promote (Phase E).
- E2e canary infra (Phase F).
- Phase A's actual fold — wait for `agent-native-runner` or a
  `test-harness` LLM-calling code path.

content.text:
Plan implementation complete. Summary:

**Phase A** — parked behind its trigger (no non-SDK runner yet).

**Phase B — MCP-skill bundling** → **PR #113** `feat(skills): MCP-skill bundling (Phase 1 follow-up B)`
- 6 commits on `worktree-skills-mcp-bundling-phase-b`
- `capabilities.mcpServers[]` manifest grammar (stdio + http, command allowlist, length caps, https-only URL, host fold)
- Orchestrator → both sandbox schemas → SDK runner + subprocess sandbox materialize per-skill `.mcp.json`
- Security-checklist note + MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE scenario
- 12 new manifest tests + 4 sandbox materialize + 6 schema rejection + 1 orch union

**Phase C — sourceUrl + version-aware refresh** → **PR #114** `feat(skills): version-aware refresh from sourceUrl (Phase 1 follow-up C)`, stacked on #113
- 5 commits on `worktree-skills-versioning-phase-c`
- `source_url` column + `sourceUrl?` threading through types/store
- Top-level `sourceUrl:` manifest grammar (https-only, HOSTNAME_RE)
- New `skills:check-for-updates` service hook + `/check-update` + `/refresh-from-source` admin routes
- SkillsTab Badge + Update button, per-skill check on mount
- Design lock-in note + MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE scenario
- 12 new tests (skills) + 4 new tests (channel-web)

All quality gates: `pnpm build` clean, `pnpm -w lint` 0 errors, targeted package tests all green. MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE walks against `kind ax-next-dev` are the outstanding manual step per `[[k8s-acceptance-loop]]`.

📝 Walkthrough ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **New Features** * Skills can now bundle and declare MCP servers using both `stdio` and `http` transports within their manifest, enabling MCP tool availability when the skill is attached to an agent. * MCP tools are dynamically made available in chat and automatically removed when the skill is detached. * **Documentation** * Added manual acceptance testing procedure for MCP-bundled skills. * Added security review notes for Phase B MCP server capability integration. ## Walkthrough This PR implements Phase B support for Model Context Protocol (MCP) server bundling within skills, enabling declarations and runtime spawning of MCP servers through a new `capabilities.mcpServers` field in skill manifests. Changes span manifest parsing, skill persistence, orchestration wiring, sandbox schema contracts, and runtime `.mcp.json` materialization in both subprocess and SDK runner execution contexts, along with comprehensive validation, test coverage, and security documentation. ## Changes **MCP Server Bundling for Skills** | Layer / File(s) | Summary | |---|---| | **Type definitions and public contracts**
`packages/skills/src/types.ts` | Introduces `McpServerSpec` interface with fields for name, transport, optional command/args/env/url, and required allowedHosts/credentials; extends `SkillCapabilities` to include `mcpServers: McpServerSpec[]` array. | | **Manifest parsing and validation**
`packages/skills/src/manifest.ts`, `packages/skills/src/__tests__/manifest*.test.ts` | Implements full YAML parsing for `capabilities.mcpServers` with stdio command allowlisting, host/URL validation, credential parsing, transport constraints, and size bounds; adds new `ManifestCode` error types for invalid MCP configuration. | | **Skill storage and admin API**
`packages/skills/src/store.ts`, `packages/skills/src/admin-routes.ts`, `packages/skills/src/__tests__/store.test.ts`, `packages/skills/src/__tests__/admin-routes.test.ts` | Persists and retrieves parsed `mcpServers` via `manifest_yaml`; extends HTTP error handling for MCP validation errors as 400 responses; verifies round-tripping through storage layer. | | **Orchestration and skill resolution**
`packages/chat-orchestrator/src/orchestrator.ts`, `packages/chat-orchestrator/src/__tests__/orchestrator.test.ts` | Threads `capabilities.mcpServers` through resolved skills; builds `installedSkillsForSandbox` payload including per-skill `mcpServers` for sandbox:open-session with defensive empty-array fallback. | | **Sandbox schema contract and validation**
`packages/sandbox-k8s/src/open-session.ts`, `packages/sandbox-k8s/src/__tests__/open-session-schema.test.ts` | Defines `McpServerSchema` validation and updates `InstalledSkillSchema` with bounded `mcpServers` array; k8s validates name regex, transport enum, args/url field constraints at boundary. | | **Sandbox and runner MCP materialization**
`packages/sandbox-subprocess/src/open-session.ts`, `packages/sandbox-subprocess/src/__tests__/open-session.test.ts`, `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/installed-skills.ts`, `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/__tests__/installed-skills.test.ts` | Materializes skill-local `.mcp.json` files alongside `SKILL.md` with read-only permissions when `mcpServers` is non-empty; translates validated specs into Anthropic SDK format for `stdio` (command/args/env) and `http` (type/url) transports; validates MCP specs at runner trust boundary. | | **UI fixtures, acceptance, and documentation**
`packages/channel-web/src/components/admin/__tests__/*.test.tsx`, `deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md`, `docs/plans/2026-05-20-skills-mcp-bundling-security-note.md`, `.gitignore` | Updates test fixtures to include `mcpServers: []` in mocked `SkillSummary`/`SkillCapabilities`; adds manual acceptance scenario for MCP-bundled skill installation/attachment/chat verification; includes security-review plan detailing capability surface, security boundaries, prompt-injection pathways, and supply-chain considerations. | ## Estimated code review effort 🎯 4 (Complex) | ⏱️ ~60 minutes ## Possibly related PRs - [project-ax/ax-next#96](https://github.com/project-ax/ax-next/pull/96): Established the installed-skills materialization workflow and sandbox orchestration foundation upon which this PR's per-skill MCP server bundling is built.

Body: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Enforce MCP `env` size/length caps at manifest-parse time.**

Line 279–291 validates `env` shape but does not enforce the same bounded contract as `args` (count/length). That allows manifests to pass here and fail later at sandbox re-validation, or persist oversized values unexpectedly. Please add entry/value bounds (32 × 256) in this parser and cover it with a regression test.

<details>
<summary>Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
 const MCP_ARGS_MAX = 32;

input.prompt:
Address the 8 CodeRabbit inline review comments on PR #113 (`feat(skills): MCP-skill bundling`). All comments cluster around a single theme: enforce transport-specific MCP invariants at every re-validation layer, plus a small env size/count cap in the manifest parser, plus a markdown lint fix.

## Working directory

`/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/skills-mcp-bundling-phase-b`

Branch: `worktree-skills-mcp-bundling-phase-b`. Working tree clean. Tests baseline pre-fix:
- `pnpm --filter @ax/skills test` → 72 passing
- `pnpm --filter @ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner test` → 190 passing
- `pnpm --filter @ax/sandbox-k8s test` → 62 passing
- `pnpm --filter @ax/sandbox-subprocess test` → 34 passing

## The 8 comments

### (1) MINOR — docs/plans/2026-05-20-skills-mcp-bundling-security-note.md:153-167

The fenced code block under "## Output contract (paste this into the PR body)" is missing a fence language (`MD040`). Change the opening fence from triple-backtick to triple-backtick + `md`:

```diff
-```
+```md
```

### (2) MAJOR — packages/skills/src/manifest.ts (around line 279-291)

Today the manifest parser caps `args` at 32 entries × 256 chars but does NOT cap `env`. A 50-key env, each value 10MB, would pass parsing and fail later. The grammar should be symmetric.

Add (next to `MCP_ARGS_MAX` / `MCP_ARG_LEN_MAX`):
```typescript
const MCP_ENV_MAX = 32;
const MCP_ENV_LEN_MAX = 256;
```

In the env-validation branch of `parseMcpServers`, enforce:
- Number of env entries ≤ `MCP_ENV_MAX`
- Each env value's string length ≤ `MCP_ENV_LEN_MAX`
- Each env KEY length ≤ `MCP_ENV_LEN_MAX` too (defensive — keys flow into the spawned process's env)

Reject with `invalid-manifest` and a specific message.

Add a regression test in `packages/skills/src/__tests__/manifest-mcp-servers.test.ts`:
- env with 33 keys → rejected
- env with a 257-char value → rejected
- env with a 257-char key → rejected

### (3) MAJOR — packages/sandbox-k8s/src/open-session.ts (around line 73-86)

`McpServerSchema` accepts:
- `transport: 'stdio'` with NO `command`
- `transport: 'http'` with NO `url`
- A stdio entry with a `url` field (cross-contamination)
- An http entry with `command`/`args`/`env` (cross-contamination)

Add a zod `.refine()` (or restructure as a discriminated union — `.refine()` is the cheaper edit) so:
- `transport === 'stdio'` → `command` is a required non-empty string AND `url` is undefined
- `transport === 'http'` → `url` is a required string AND `command`/`args`/`env` are all undefined

Pattern (adapt to existing zod version in the project):
```typescript
const McpServerSchema = z.object({
  name: z.string().regex(...),
  transport: z.enum(['stdio', 'http']),
  command: z.string().optional(),
  args: z.array(z.string().max(256)).max(32).optional(),
  env: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
  url: z.string().url().optional(),
  allowedHosts: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
  credentials: z.array(z.object({ slot: z.string(), kind: z.literal('api-key') })).default([]),
}).refine(
  (v) => {
    if (v.transport === 'stdio') {
      return typeof v.command === 'string' && v.command.length > 0 && v.url === undefined;
    }
    // http
    return typeof v.url === 'string' && v.command === undefined && v.args === undefined && v.env === undefined;
  },
  { message: 'mcpServers entry must match its transport: stdio requires command (no url); http requires url (no command/args/env)' },
);
```

### (4) MAJOR — packages/sandbox-subprocess/src/open-session.ts (around line 92-104)

Same change as (3) — the subprocess sandbox carries the structural twin schema. Apply the same `.refine()` rule.

### (5) MAJOR — packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/installed-skills.ts (in `validateMcpEntry`, around line 80-116)

`validateMcpEntry` re-validates the AX_INSTALLED_SKILLS_JSON payload at the trust boundary. Today it allows stdio without command and http without url. Add the same transport-conditional checks BEFORE the helper returns:

After parsing fields, before returning, add:
```typescript
if (v.transport === 'stdio') {
  if (typeof v.command !== 'string' || v.command.length === 0) {
    throw new Error(`mcpServers entry '${v.name}' (stdio) is missing required 'command'`);
  }
  if (v.url !== undefined) {
    throw new Error(`mcpServers entry '${v.name}' (stdio) must not set 'url'`);
  }
} else {
  // http
  if (typeof v.url !== 'string' || v.url.length === 0) {
    throw new Error(`mcpServers entry '${v.name}' (http) is missing required 'url'`);
  }
  if (v.command !== undefined || v.args !== undefined || v.env !== undefined) {
    throw new Error(`mcpServers entry '${v.name}' (http) must not set 'command', 'args', or 'env'`);
  }
}
```

Also add an args length cap (same pattern as sandbox schemas):
```typescript
if (Array.isArray(v.args)) {
  if (v.args.length > 32) throw new Error(`mcpServers entry '${v.name}' has too many args`);
  if (!v.args.every((a) => typeof a === 'string' && a.length <= 256)) {
    throw new Error(`mcpServers entry '${v.name}' has an arg over 256 chars`);
  }
}
```

If env validation already exists, extend with the same caps (32 entries × 256 chars on values + keys).

Match the existing code style — look at how the file currently throws (probably `throw new Error(...)`).

### (6) MAJOR — packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/__tests__/installed-skills.test.ts

Add tests proving the new rejection paths:
- stdio entry without command throws
- http entry without url throws
- args over 32 entries throws
- args with a >256-char string throws
- stdio entry with a url field throws (cross-contamination)
- http entry with a command field throws (cross-contamination)

Match the existing test pattern (set `process.env['AX_INSTALLED_SKILLS_JSON']` then call `materializeInstalledSkillsFromEnv()` and `expect(...).rejects.toThrow(...)`).

### (7) MAJOR — packages/sandbox-k8s/src/__tests__/open-session-schema.test.ts

Add rejection cases to the existing tests:
- `transport: 'stdio'` with missing `command` → safeParse fails
- `transport: 'stdio'` with empty `command` → safeParse fails
- `transport: 'http'` with missing `url` → safeParse fails
- `transport: 'stdio'` with both `command` and `url` set → safeParse fails (cross-contamination)
- `transport: 'http'` with both `url` and `command` set → safeParse fails

Match the existing one-field-mutation pattern (the file has a `validBaseInput()` helper).

### (8) MAJOR — packages/sandbox-subprocess/src/__tests__/open-session.test.ts

Add negative tests for `sandbox:open-session` (the existing tests already exercise the schema indirectly via the open-session call). Add tests asserting that calling open-session with installedSkills containing:
- a stdio mcpServers entry with no command, OR
- an http mcpServers entry with no url
fails with `invalid-payload` (or whatever error code the existing schema-rejection tests use — check the file).

If those tests are easier to write as a direct `OpenSessionInputSchema.safeParse(...)` call (mirroring the k8s schema test), do that — but the open-session.test.ts file already has the harness scaffolding, so direct schema tests can sit next to the existing failure-mode tests.

## After fixes

Run:
```
pnpm --filter @ax/skills test 2>&1 | tail -10
pnpm --filter @ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner test 2>&1 | tail -10
pnpm --filter @ax/sandbox-k8s test 2>&1 | tail -10
pnpm --filter @ax/sandbox-subprocess test 2>&1 | tail -10
pnpm build 2>&1 | tail -5
pnpm -w lint 2>&1 | tail -5
```

All green. Pre-existing 2 lint warnings should remain (they're unrelated).

## Commit

Single follow-up commit (don't amend):
```
git add packages/skills/src packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src packages/sandbox-k8s/src packages/sandbox-subprocess/src docs/plans/2026-05-20-skills-mcp-bundling-security-note.md
git commit -m "test(skills): tighten transport-specific MCP invariants + env caps (PR #113 review fixes)"
git push
```

## Report back

- Status: DONE / BLOCKED
- Per-file summary
- Test counts before/after for each touched package
- New commit SHA
- Anything surprising (especially if zod refine doesn't compose with the existing schema usage, or if existing tests break unexpectedly)

If any of the existing tests broke as a result of tightening the invariants (e.g., a Phase B test passed a stdio entry without command because it didn't matter at the time), STOP and report BLOCKED with the specific test + its expected fix.
